title: Whole-Body Motion Tracking for Unitree G1
description: A guided tour of the motion tracking system that trains, evaluates, and deploys whole-body motion tracking policies for the Unitree G1 humanoid robot, with compliance-aware behavior and VR teleoperation support.

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: This codebase implements a full pipeline for humanoid robot motion tracking: from training reinforcement learning policies in simulation (active_adaptation/) to deploying them on real hardware (sim2real/). Configuration is managed through Hydra YAML files (cfg/), and standalone scripts handle data processing and evaluation (scripts/).

steps.title: Training & Simulation Core
steps.description: The active_adaptation/ directory is the heart of the system. It contains the RL environment (envs/), the PPO learning algorithm (learning/), robot model assets (assets/), and utility modules for motion processing, kinematics, and experiment tracking (utils/). Training runs across multiple GPUs using TorchRL and mjlab for MuJoCo physics simulation.

steps.title: Environment & MDP Structure
steps.description: The environment is built on mjlab and follows a modular MDP (Markov Decision Process) structure. base.py wraps the physics simulator with observation groups and action application. The mdp/ subdirectory breaks down the RL problem into composable pieces: observations, rewards, randomizations, terminations, actions, and motion tracking commands with compliance awareness.

steps.title: MDP Components Deep Dive
steps.description: Each MDP component is a self-contained module. observations.py extracts policy inputs (gravity, joint history, compliance flags). rewards/locomotion.py defines multi-objective rewards for tracking accuracy and locomotion quality. randomizations.py applies domain randomization (friction, mass, motor params) for sim-to-real transfer. commands/motion_tracking.py manages reference motion trajectories with compliance detection.

steps.title: PPO Learning Algorithm
steps.description: The learning system implements Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with distributed multi-GPU support. ppo.py contains the core training loop with GAE advantage estimation and value normalization. common.py defines the neural network architecture (MLP-based policy and value networks). Supporting modules handle custom distributions, ensemble methods, and uncertainty estimation via evidential learning.

steps.title: Motion Processing & Kinematics
steps.description: The utils/ directory handles motion data and kinematics. motion.py defines MotionData and MotionDataset classes for lazy-loading memory-mapped motion clips. fk_helper.py computes forward kinematics for body part positions. multimotion.py manages multi-clip datasets mixing AMASS, LAFAN, 100style, and real VR captures. wandb.py integrates W&B for experiment tracking and checkpoint management.

steps.title: Hydra Configuration System
steps.description: Training is configured through a hierarchical Hydra YAML system. train.yaml is the entry point. Task configs in task/G1/ define the full MDP: action space, observation space, reward functions, randomization, and dataset mixing. Experiment configs in exp/ override parameters for each training phase (train -> adapt -> finetune), enabling a 3-phase curriculum of 8B + 1B + 1B frames.

steps.title: Sim2Real Deployment
steps.description: The sim2real/ directory is a standalone runtime for deploying trained policies. deploy.py is the main controller running at 50Hz. policy.py wraps ONNX inference. Motion can come from UDP (offline clips via motion_select.py) or VR teleoperation (via teleop/ ZMQ bridge). sim2sim.py tests the policy in MuJoCo before going to real hardware. The common/ directory handles joint mapping between simulator and real robot coordinate spaces.

steps.title: Training Scripts & Data Pipeline
steps.description: scripts/train.py is the main training entry point, launched via torchrun for multi-GPU training. scripts/eval.py handles evaluation and ONNX export. The data_process/ directory converts retargeted motion captures (AMASS, LAFAN) into memory-mapped training datasets. train.sh orchestrates the full 3-phase training pipeline: train (8B frames) -> adapt (1B) -> finetune (1B).

steps.title: The Complete Pipeline
steps.description: The full workflow: (1) Process motion capture datasets into training data, (2) Train PPO policies in mjlab simulation with domain randomization across 3 phases, (3) Export trained policies to ONNX, (4) Test in sim2sim, (5) Deploy to the real Unitree G1 robot at 50Hz with either offline motion clips or live VR teleoperation. The compliance-aware system enables safe, contact-rich whole-body interaction.
